summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a tight spring‑90 show highlighted by Candyman, Ship of Fools and a stellar To Lay Me Down
review: Fans rave about the pristine SBD mix – crystal clear, tight and often called the best‑sounding Dead recording of the era. The performance is equally praised; the band was “at the peak” with standout moments on Candyman, Ship of Fools, To Lay Me Down, Stella Blue, Baby Blue and a ripping Truckin', while China Rider draws mixed opinions.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews consistently praise both the musical performance and the sound quality; only minor mixed comments on one song keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, tight mix praised by multiple reviewers as the best‑sounding GD SBD of the late‑80s/early‑90s; no significant drop‑outs or muffiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, well‑structured set with a strong first half and an especially smoking second set; transitions praised, overall pacing described as “tight” and “very strong.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly noted for superb guitar tone and soaring solos; one reviewer called his playing ‘splendid’ despite a bewildered vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keys) described as “rockin the whole show,” “hot again here,” and “ripping it” – a major highlight of the night.
